title: SCPI Backus-Naur Form (BNF) Notation Reference
body: SCPI commands use Backus-Naur Form (BNF) notation to define syntax. The BNF symbols are: < > = Defined element. | = Exclusive OR (choose one). { } = Group; one element is required. [ ] = Optional; can be omitted. ... = Previous element(s) may be repeated. Example: {OFF|ON|<NR1>} means you must provide either OFF, ON, or a signed integer. [<NR1>] means the numeric argument is optional. These symbols appear in programmer manuals and command syntax definitions throughout Tektronix documentation.

title: SCPI Command vs Query Structure
body: SCPI commands come in two forms: Set commands modify instrument settings or perform actions. Query commands return data and status information and always end with '?'. Many commands support both forms (commandType: 'both'). Example set: ACQuire:MODe SAMple. Example query: ACQuire:MODe? A command header consists of mnemonics separated by colons. Arguments are separated from the header by a space. Multiple arguments are separated by commas. The header hierarchy defines the command tree structure.

title: SCPI Message Elements: Header, Mnemonic, Argument
body: SCPI message elements: <Header> = Basic command name; if it ends with '?' it is a query. <Mnemonic> = Header subfunction; multiple mnemonics are separated by ':' (colon). <Argument> = Quantity, quality, restriction, or limit associated with the header; separated from header by <Space>. <Comma> = Separator between multiple arguments. <Space> = White space character(s) between command header and first argument. Example: 'ACQuire:SEQuence:NUMSEQuence 2' has header 'ACQuire:SEQuence:NUMSEQuence', three mnemonics ['ACQuire','SEQuence','NUMSEQuence'], and argument '2'.

title: SCPI Command Abbreviation and Case Rules
body: SCPI commands use mixed-case mnemonics where UPPERCASE letters indicate the minimum required abbreviation. The lowercase portion is optional. Example: 'ACQuire:NUMAvg' can be entered as 'ACQ:NUMA' or 'acquire:numavg' or 'ACQuire:NUMAvg'. Commands are case-insensitive. Commands can be preceded by white space characters. The instrument ignores commands consisting only of white space and line feeds. Recommendation: Use the full spelling (e.g., 'ACQuire:NUMAvg') for most robust and readable automation code.

title: SCPI Command Concatenation with Semicolons
body: Multiple SCPI commands can be concatenated using semicolons (;) for efficiency. Rules: (1) Separate commands with different root headers using ';:' — e.g., 'TRIGger:A:MODe NORMal;:ACQuire:NUMAVg 8'. (2) Commands sharing the same root header can omit the root — e.g., 'ACQuire:MODe ENVelope;NUMAVg 8' (both under ACQuire). (3) Never precede IEEE 488.2 common commands (*RST, *OPC, etc.) with a colon — e.g., 'ACQuire:STATE 1;*OPC'. (4) Concatenated queries return concatenated responses — e.g., query 'DISplay:GRAticule?;STYle?' returns 'FULL;DOTSONLY'.

title: SCPI Message Termination (EOM, EOI, LF)
body: SCPI message termination: Messages must end with an END message (EOI asserted with last data byte). The last data byte may optionally be an ASCII line feed (LF, 0x0A). The instrument always terminates outgoing messages with LF and EOI. For socket connections, terminate commands with '\n' (newline). For VISA, the library handles termination automatically. Use Device Clear (DCL) to clear the output queue and reset the parser if communication gets out of sync.

title: SCPI Numeric Argument Types: NR1, NR2, NR3
body: SCPI numeric argument types: <NR1> = Signed integer value (e.g., 8, -5, 100). <NR2> = Floating point value without exponent (e.g., 3.14, -0.5). <NR3> = Floating point value with exponent / scientific notation (e.g., 1.5E-6, 2.5E+3). <bin> = Signed or unsigned integer in binary format. IMPORTANT: Invalid numeric values are automatically forced to valid settings by rounding or truncating — the instrument does NOT return an error. A query returning 9.91E+37 indicates NaN (Not a Number) error condition.

title: SCPI Non-Numeric Argument Types: Enumeration, QString, Block
body: SCPI non-numeric argument types: Enumeration = Unquoted text words where uppercase portion is required, lowercase optional (e.g., SAMple, ENVelope, NORMal). <QString> = Quoted string of ASCII text, max 1000 characters. Use matching quotes to open/close. Include a quote character by doubling it (e.g., '"here is a "" mark"'). Case-insensitive. <Block> = Block of binary data bytes, prefixed with #<NZDig><Dig...> header indicating byte count. Block format: #<n><length><data> where <n> is number of digits in <length>.

title: SCPI Constructed Mnemonics: CH<x>, REF<x>, MATH<x>, MEAS<x>
body: SCPI constructed mnemonics use <x> as a numeric placeholder: CH<x> = Channel specifier (x >= 1, limited by instrument channel count). CH<x>_D<x> = Digital channel specifier. REF<x> = Reference waveform specifier (x >= 1). REF<x>_D<x> = Digital reference waveform. MATH<x> = Math waveform specifier (x >= 1). MEAS<x> = Measurement specifier (x >= 1). B<x> = Bus specifier (x >= 1). CURSOR<x> = Cursor selector (x = 1 or 2). SEARCH<x> = Search specifier (x >= 1). WAVEView<x> = Waveview (x = 1). PLOTView<x> = Plotview (x = 1). ZOOM<x> = Zoom (x = 1). Example: 'CH1:SCAle 0.5' sets channel 1 vertical scale.

title: SCPI Error Handling and Special Values
body: SCPI error handling: Invalid numeric values are automatically rounded or truncated to valid settings (no error generated). A query returning 9.91E+37 indicates NaN (Not a Number) error condition. Use *ESR? to read the Event Status Register and check for errors. Use ALLEV? to read all events from the event queue. Use *CLS to clear the status registers and error queue. Use Device Clear (DCL) to clear the output queue and reset the command parser. Always check *ESR? after critical operations to detect command errors, execution errors, or query errors.

title: SCPI Pattern: Set Command with Numeric Argument
body: Pattern for SCPI set command with numeric argument. Structure: <Header> <NR1|NR2|NR3>. Example: 'BUS:B1:AUDIO:DATA:SIZE 8' — header is 'BUS:B1:AUDIO:DATA:SIZE', argument is 8 (type NR1). Example: 'HORizontal:SCAle 1.0E-6' — sets horizontal scale to 1 microsecond/division (type NR3). Example: 'CH1:SCAle 0.5' — sets channel 1 scale to 0.5 volts/division (type NR2). The instrument rounds or truncates invalid values to the nearest valid setting.

title: SCPI Pattern: Set Command with Enumeration Argument
body: Pattern for SCPI set command with enumeration argument. Structure: <Header> <Enumeration>. Example: 'ACQuire:MODe SAMple' — sets acquisition mode to Sample. Example: 'TRIGger:A:MODe NORMal' — sets trigger mode to Normal. Enumerations are case-insensitive and can be abbreviated to uppercase portion. 'SAMPLE', 'SAMple', and 'sample' are all equivalent. Common enumerations: ON/OFF, {0|1}, NORMal/INVErted, RUN/STOP, SAMple/PEAKdetect/HIRes/AVErage/ENVelope.

title: SCPI Pattern: Query Command and Response Parsing
body: Pattern for SCPI query commands. Structure: <Header>? — append '?' to the set header. Response formats: Numeric queries return NR1/NR2/NR3 as ASCII text (e.g., '1', '0.500000', '1.0E-6'). Enumeration queries return the full keyword (e.g., 'SAMPLE', 'NORMAL'). String queries return quoted strings. Use header ON/OFF (*HDR ON or *HDR OFF / HEADer ON or OFF) to control whether the response includes the command header. With header off: 'ACQuire:MODe?' returns 'SAMPLE'. With header on: returns ':ACQuire:MODe SAMPLE'.

title: IEEE 488.2 Common Commands (*IDN?, *RST, *OPC?, *CLS, *ESR?)
body: IEEE 488.2 common commands begin with asterisk (*) and work on all SCPI instruments: *IDN? = Identification query; returns manufacturer, model, serial, firmware (e.g., 'TEKTRONIX,MSO68B,B012682,CF:91.1CT FV:2.20.8'). *RST = Reset instrument to factory defaults. *CLS = Clear status registers and error queue. *OPC? = Operation Complete query; returns '1' when all pending operations finish (use after SAVE, EXPort, etc.). *OPC = Operation Complete command; sets OPC bit in ESR when done. *ESR? = Event Status Register query. *WAI = Wait for all pending operations. *TST? = Self-test query. Never precede * commands with a colon in concatenated sequences.

title: SCPI Dynamic Activation: Implicit Object Creation
body: Some SCPI commands implicitly create objects when queried or set. This is called dynamic activation. Measurement example: Querying 'MEASUrement:MEAS1:TYPe?' may create measurement MEAS1 if it doesn't exist, with a default type (e.g., PERIod). Math example: Setting 'MATH1:DEFine "CH1+CH2"' creates MATH1 if not present. Bus example: Setting 'BUS:B1:TYPE CAN' creates bus B1. This means automation scripts should be aware that simply querying a non-existent measurement/math/bus may create it with default settings. Use MEASUrement:DELete to remove unwanted measurements.

title: SCPI Waveform Data Transfer Pattern
body: Waveform transfer uses the Waveform Transfer command group. Setup sequence: (1) DATa:SOURce CH1 — select source waveform. (2) DATa:ENCdg RIBinary — set encoding (ASCII, RIBinary, SRIBinary, RFBinary, SRFBinary). (3) DATa:WIDth 2 — set bytes per data point (1 or 2). (4) DATa:STARt 1; DATa:STOP 10000 — set record range. (5) WFMOutpre? — query waveform preamble (scaling factors). (6) CURVe? — transfer waveform data. Binary data uses IEEE 488.2 block format: #<n><length><data>. Use preamble values (YMUlt, YOFf, YZEro, XINcr, XZEro) to convert raw ADC values to physical units: voltage = (raw - YOFf) * YMUlt + YZEro.

title: SCPI File System Commands: READFile, DELEte, CWD, MKDIR
body: File System commands manage files on the instrument's internal storage. Key commands: FILESystem:CWD? — query current working directory. FILESystem:CWD "path" — set working directory. FILESystem:MKDIR "path" — create directory. FILESystem:READFile "path" — transfer file contents from instrument to controller (returns raw binary data, NO IEEE 488.2 block header). FILESystem:DELEte "path" — delete file. FILESystem:COPy "source","dest" — copy file. FILESystem:DIR? — list directory contents. Common paths: MSO 4/5/6 series uses 'C:/Temp' or 'C:/Users/Public/Tektronix/TekScope'. MSO/DPO 70000 series uses 'C:/TekScope'.

title: Tektronix Scope Families: Modern MSO 4/5/6/7 Series Overview
body: Modern Tektronix MSO oscilloscopes (4/5/6/7 Series) share a common SCPI command set with 34 command groups and approximately 2,952 commands. Models include MSO44, MSO44B, MSO46B, MSO54, MSO56B, MSO58, MSO58B, MSO58LP, MSO64, MSO64B, MSO66B, MSO68B, and LPD64. These scopes use the SAVE:IMAGe command for screenshots (NOT EXPort or HARDCopy). The firmware version can be queried with *IDN? and appears as 'FV:x.xx.x'. The programmer manual is 'MSO 4/5/6 Series Programmer Manual' (077189801). Some commands require specific options (e.g., 5-DPM for Digital Power Management, AFG for Arbitrary Function Generator).

title: Tektronix Scope Families: Legacy MSO/DPO 5k/7k/70k Series Overview
body: Legacy Tektronix oscilloscopes include MSO/DPO5000/B, DPO7000/C, DPO70000/B/C/D/DX/SX, DSA70000/B/C/D, and MSO70000/C/DX Series. These scopes use the EXPort command set for screenshots (NOT SAVE:IMAGe). The HARDCopy commands are also available on these platforms. Key model identifiers: MSO5xxx/DPO5xxx (5000 series), DPO7xxx (7000 series), MSO7xxxx/DPO7xxxx (70000 series). The programmer manual is 'MSO/DPO5000, DPO7000, DPO70000 Series Programmer Manual' (077001026). These scopes have different command groups and counts compared to modern scopes.

title: Tektronix AFG Series: Arbitrary Function Generator SCPI Overview
body: Tektronix AFG (Arbitrary Function Generator) series includes AFG31K, AFG3KB, AFG3KC, AFG3K models. AFG commands use a different command tree from oscilloscopes. Key command groups: Output (OUTPut:STATe, OUTPut:IMPedance), Waveform (SOURce:FUNCtion:SHAPe), Frequency (SOURce:FREQuency:FIXed), Amplitude (SOURce:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:AMPLitude), Phase (SOURce:PHASe:ADJust), Modulation (SOURce:AM/FM/PM), Burst (SOURce:BURSt), Sweep (SOURce:FREQuency:MODE), Trigger (TRIGger:SEQuence), System (*IDN?, *RST). Channel specifier uses {ch} = 1 or 2. Manual: AFG31000 Series Programmer Manual (077-1115-00).

title: TekExpress Compliance Testing SCPI Overview
body: TekExpress is Tektronix's compliance testing automation software with its own SCPI command interface. All commands use the TEKEXP: prefix. Key commands: TEKEXP:SELECT DEVICE,"<name>" — select DUT. TEKEXP:SELECT SUITE,"<name>" — select test suite. TEKEXP:SELECT TEST,"<name>",<0|1> — enable/disable test. TEKEXP:VALUE GENERAL,"<param>","<value>" — set general parameters. TEKEXP:LIST? DEVICE/SUITE/TEST/VERSION — query available options. TEKEXP:INSTRUMENT,"<type>","<ip>" — configure instrument connections. TEKEXP:STATE RUN — start testing. TEKEXP:STATE? — query run state. TEKEXP:RESULT? — query test results. Applications include USB4Tx, PCIe, Ethernet compliance.

title: Screenshot Command Differences Across Tektronix Families
body: Screenshot commands differ significantly across Tektronix instrument families. MSO 4/5/6 Series (Modern): Use 'SAVE:IMAGe "path.png"' — format is determined by file extension (.png/.bmp/.jpg). Optional setup: SAVE:IMAGe:VIEWTYpe FULLScreen, SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition NORMal. EXPort and HARDCopy commands DO NOT WORK on these scopes. MSO/DPO 70000 Series (Legacy): Use 'EXPort START' — requires separate setup: EXPort:FILEName, EXPort:FORMat, EXPort:VIEW, EXPort:PALEtte. SAVE:IMAGe DOES NOT WORK on these scopes. Also supports HARDCopy START as alternative trigger. MSO/DPO 5k/7k Series: Use HARDCopy commands — HARDCOPY:DATA? for direct stream, or HARDCOPY PORT FILE + HARDCOPY START for file-based transfer.

title: PI Command Translator: Legacy to Modern Command Migration
body: Modern Tektronix oscilloscopes (firmware v1.30+) include a built-in Programming Interface (PI) Command Translator that automatically converts legacy commands to modern equivalents. This allows existing automation scripts written for DPO7000, MSO/DPO5000 series to work on MSO 4/5/6 Series without code changes. The translator handles differences in command trees, argument formats, and response formats. Use this for migration scenarios but note: (1) Not all legacy commands have modern equivalents. (2) Performance may be slightly reduced due to translation overhead. (3) New scripts should use native modern commands. (4) The translator can be enabled/disabled in instrument settings.

title: Binary Data Transfer: FILESystem:READFile Returns Raw Bytes
body: CRITICAL: Both MSO 4/5/6 and MSO/DPO 70000 series return RAW BINARY data from FILESystem:READFile — there is NO IEEE 488.2 block header (#<n><length><data>). The data is pure image bytes (PNG/BMP/JPEG). PNG files start with magic bytes: 89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A. PyVISA: Standard scope.read_raw() may timeout because it waits for termination. Use visalib.read() directly with 65536-byte chunks and check StatusCode.success_max_count_read to detect more data available. Raw socket: Use recv(65536) with 5-second timeout per chunk; when timeout occurs with data already received, transfer is complete. Always strip trailing newline if present.

title: Command Group: Acquisition (15 commands)
body: The Acquisition command group controls how the instrument acquires signals and processes them into waveforms. Key commands: ACQuire:STATE {RUN|STOP|1|0} — start/stop acquisitions. ACQuire:MODe {SAMple|PEAKdetect|HIRes|AVErage|ENVelope} — set acquisition mode. ACQuire:NUMAVg <NR1> — set number of averages (when in AVErage mode). ACQuire:STOPAfter {RUNSTop|SEQuence} — set stop condition. ACQuire:SEQuence:NUMSEQuence <NR1> — set number of acquisitions in sequence mode. ACQuire:ENHANCEDEnob {OFF|AUTO} — enhanced effective number of bits. Typical workflow: Set mode and parameters, then start acquisition with ACQuire:STATE RUN.

title: Command Group: Trigger (266-298 commands)
body: The Trigger command group controls all triggering aspects. There are two triggers: A (main) and B (delayed). Key commands: TRIGger:A:TYPe {EDGE|PULSEWidth|TIMEOUT|RUNT|WINdow|LOGIc|SETHold|BUS} — set trigger type. TRIGger:A:EDGE:SOURce {CH<x>|AUX|LINE} — set edge trigger source. TRIGger:A:EDGE:SLOPe {RISe|FALL|EITher} — set edge slope. TRIGger:A:LEVel:CH<x> <NR3> — set trigger level. TRIGger:A:MODe {AUTO|NORMal} — set trigger mode. TRIGger:FORCe — force a trigger event. The trigger group is one of the largest, with extensive bus protocol trigger support (CAN, I2C, SPI, USB, Ethernet, ARINC429, FlexRay, LIN, MIL-STD-1553B).

title: Command Group: Measurement (65-367 commands)
body: The Measurement command group controls automated measurements. Key commands: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:TYPe {FREQuency|PERIod|MEAN|PK2Pk|RMS|RISe|FALL|AMPlitude|...} — set measurement type. MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:SOUrce CH<x> — set measurement source. MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:VALue? — query measurement result. MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:CURRentacq:MEAN? — query current acquisition mean. MEASUrement:ADDNew "MEAS<x>" — add new measurement. MEASUrement:DELete "MEAS<x>" — delete measurement. MEASUrement:ANNOTation — control measurement annotations. Measurements can be assigned different parameters (sources, reference levels) independently.

title: Command Group: Display, Horizontal, Vertical
body: Display commands (130 cmds) control instrument display: DISplay:WAVEView1:CH<x>:STATE {ON|OFF} — show/hide channel. DISplay:GRAticule:TYPe {FULL|GRID|CROSShair|FRAMe} — set graticule. DISplay:PERSistence {OFF|INFPersist|VARpersist} — set persistence. Horizontal commands (48-54 cmds) control time base: HORizontal:SCAle <NR3> — set time/division. HORizontal:POSition <NR3> — set horizontal position. HORizontal:RECOrdlength <NR1> — set record length. HORizontal:MODE {AUTO|MANual|CONStant} — set mode. Vertical: CH<x>:SCAle <NR3> — set volts/division. CH<x>:POSition <NR3> — set vertical position. CH<x>:COUPling {DC|AC|GND} — set coupling. CH<x>:BANdwidth <NR3> — set bandwidth limit.

title: Command Group: Bus Protocol Configuration (85-339 commands)
body: Bus commands configure serial protocol decoding. Key commands: BUS:B<x>:TYPe {CAN|LIN|SPI|I2C|RS232C|FLEXRAY|USB|ETHERNET|ARINC429A|MIL1553B|...} — set bus type. CAN example: BUS:B1:TYPe CAN; BUS:B1:CAN:BITRate 500000; BUS:B1:CAN:SOURce CH1. I2C example: BUS:B1:TYPe I2C; BUS:B1:I2C:SCLk:SOURce CH1; BUS:B1:I2C:SDAta:SOURce CH2. SPI example: BUS:B1:TYPe SPI; BUS:B1:SPI:SCLk:SOURce CH1; BUS:B1:SPI:SS:SOURce CH2; BUS:B1:SPI:DATA:IN:SOURce CH3. Bus configuration requires specifying type, bit rate (where applicable), and signal source assignments.

title: Command Group: Save/Recall, File System, Hard Copy
body: Save and Recall (26 cmds): SAVE:SETUp "path" — save instrument setup. RECAll:SETUp "path" — recall setup. SAVE:WAVEform CH<x>,"path" — save waveform data. File System (10-19 cmds): FILESystem:CWD? — query working directory. FILESystem:READFile "path" — read file (binary transfer). FILESystem:DELEte "path" — delete file. FILESystem:MKDIR "path" — create directory. FILESystem:DIR? — list directory. Hard Copy (11 cmds, legacy): EXPort:FILEName "path", EXPort:FORMat {PNG|BMP|JPEG|TIFF}, EXPort:VIEW {FULLSCREEN|GRATICULE|FULLNO}, EXPort:PALEtte {COLOR|BLACKWHITE}, EXPort START — trigger export. HARDCopy START — alternative trigger.

title: Screenshot Workflow: MSO 4/5/6 Series (SAVE:IMAGe Method)
body: Complete screenshot workflow for MSO 4/5/6 Series (MSO44, MSO54, MSO58, MSO64, MSO68B, LPD64, TekscopeSW). Verified on MSO68B FW 2.20.8, TekscopeSW FW 2.16.9. Step 1: SAVE:IMAGe:VIEWTYpe FULLScreen (optional — set full screen capture). Step 2: SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition NORMal (optional — set normal colors; INVErted for white background). Step 3: SAVE:IMAGe "C:/Temp/screenshot.png" (trigger — format determined by file extension .png/.bmp/.jpg). Step 4: *OPC? (wait for completion, returns '1'). Step 5: FILESystem:READFile "C:/Temp/screenshot.png" (transfer raw binary). Step 6: FILESystem:DELEte "C:/Temp/screenshot.png" (cleanup). Default working directory: C:/Users/Public/Tektronix/TekScope.

title: Screenshot Workflow: MSO/DPO 70000 Series (EXPort Method)
body: Complete screenshot workflow for MSO/DPO 70000 Series (MSO73304DX, MSO72004C, DPO70404C, etc.). Verified on MSO73304DX FW 10.14.1. Step 1: EXPort:FILEName "C:/TekScope/screenshot.png" (set output path). Step 2: EXPort:FORMat PNG (set format: PNG/BMP/TIFF/JPEG). Step 3: EXPort:VIEW FULLSCREEN (set view mode). Step 4: EXPort:PALEtte COLOR (set palette: COLOR/BLACKWHITE). Step 5: EXPort START (trigger the screenshot — MUST use 'START' not 'STARt'). Step 6: *OPC? (wait for completion). Step 7: FILESystem:READFile "C:/TekScope/screenshot.png" (transfer raw binary). Step 8: FILESystem:DELEte "C:/TekScope/screenshot.png" (cleanup). Default working directory: C:/TekScope.

title: Screenshot Workflow: MSO/DPO 5k/7k Series (HARDCopy Methods)
body: Screenshot methods for MSO/DPO 5000/B and DPO 7000/C Series. Method 1 — HARDCOPY:DATA? (fastest, direct stream): scope.write('HARDCopy:FORMat PNG'); scope.write('HARDCopy:LAYout PORTrait'); image_data = scope.query_binary_values('HARDCopy:DATA?', datatype='B', container=bytes). Method 2 — HARDCOPY PORT FILE (save + transfer): HARDCOPY:PORT FILE; HARDCOPY:FORMAT PNG; HARDCOPY:FILENAME "C:/TekScope/Temp/screenshot.png"; HARDCOPY START; time.sleep(1.0); FILESYSTEM:READFILE "C:/TekScope/Temp/screenshot.png" (transfer); FILESYSTEM:DELETE (cleanup). IMPORTANT: Use time.sleep(1.0) after HARDCOPY START, NOT *OPC?, to avoid UnicodeDecodeError from PNG binary data in buffer.

title: SAVE:IMAGe Commands — Verified Working (MSO 4/5/6)
body: Verified WORKING screenshot commands on MSO 4/5/6 Series (tested MSO68B FW 2.20.8, TekscopeSW FW 2.16.9): SAVE:IMAGe "C:/Temp/file.png" — saves screenshot, format from extension (✓). SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition? — returns NORMAL or INVERTED (✓). SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition NORMal — set normal colors (✓). SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition INVErted — set inverted/white background (✓). SAVE:IMAGe:VIEWTYpe? — returns FULLSCREEN (✓). SAVE:IMAGe:VIEWTYpe FULLScreen — set full screen capture (✓). FILESystem:CWD? — returns current directory (✓). FILESystem:READFile "path" — transfer file as raw binary (✓). FILESystem:DELEte "path" — delete file (✓). *OPC? — returns 1 when SAVE:IMAGe completes (✓).

title: SAVE:IMAGe Commands — NOT Working / Not Found (MSO 4/5/6)
body: Commands verified NOT WORKING on MSO 4/5/6 Series (Command not found errors): SAVE:IMAGe:FILEFormat? — NOT SUPPORTED (use file extension in filename instead). SAVE:IMAGe:FILEFormat PNG — NOT SUPPORTED (format is determined by .png/.bmp/.jpg extension). SAVE:IMAGe:INKSaver? — NOT SUPPORTED. SAVE:IMAGe:INKSaver OFF — NOT SUPPORTED. SAVE:IMAGe:LAYout? — NOT SUPPORTED. EXPort commands — NOT SUPPORTED on MSO 4/5/6 (use SAVE:IMAGe instead). HARDCopy commands — NOT SUPPORTED on MSO 4/5/6 (use SAVE:IMAGe instead). If you see 'command not found' errors for screenshot commands, check which scope family you're targeting.

title: EXPort Commands — Verified Working (MSO/DPO 70000)
body: Verified WORKING screenshot commands on MSO/DPO 70000 Series (tested MSO73304DX FW 10.14.1): EXPort? — returns all settings (✓). EXPort:FILEName "path" — set output path (✓). EXPort:FILEName? — query output path (✓). EXPort:FORMat PNG — set format: PNG/BMP/TIFF/JPEG (✓). EXPort:FORMat? — query format (✓). EXPort:VIEW FULLSCREEN — set view mode (✓). EXPort:VIEW? — query view mode (✓). EXPort:PALEtte COLOR — set palette: COLOR/BLACKWHITE (✓). EXPort:PALEtte? — query palette (✓). EXPort START — trigger screenshot (✓). HARDCopy START — alternative trigger (✓). FILESystem:CWD? — returns 'C:/TekScope' (✓). FILESystem:READFile "path" — transfer file (✓). *OPC? — returns 1 when complete (✓).

title: EXPort/HARDCopy Commands — NOT Working (MSO/DPO 70000)
body: Commands verified NOT WORKING on MSO/DPO 70000 Series: SAVE:IMAGe "path" — NOT SUPPORTED (use EXPort instead). SAVE:IMAGe:FILEFormat — NOT SUPPORTED (use EXPort:FORMat). HARDCopy:DATA? — NOT SUPPORTED on 70000 series. EXPort (no parameter) — MUST use 'EXPort START', not just 'EXPort'. EXPort:STARt — CASE SENSITIVE: must use 'START' not 'STARt'. Also NOT WORKING on MSO 4/5/6: EXPort commands do not exist on modern scopes. Common mistake: Trying EXPort commands on MSO5/6 series or SAVE:IMAGe on 70000 series. Always detect scope family first with *IDN?.

title: Auto-Detecting Scope Family from *IDN? Response
body: To automatically select the correct screenshot method, parse the *IDN? response. Response format: 'TEKTRONIX,<model>,<serial>,CF:91.1CT FV:<firmware>'. Detection logic: Split by comma, check model (parts[1]). If model starts with 'MSO7' or 'DPO7' → MSO/DPO 70000 series (use EXPort START). If model starts with 'MSO4' or 'MSO5' or 'MSO6' or contains 'TEKSCOPESW' → MSO 4/5/6 series (use SAVE:IMAGe). If model starts with 'MSO5' and is 4-digit (MSO5034) → MSO 5000 series (use HARDCopy). Examples: 'TEKTRONIX,MSO68B,...' → mso456. 'TEKTRONIX,MSO73304DX,...' → mso70k. 'TEKTRONIX,DPO7254C,...' → mso70k (DPO7000 uses same commands as 70000).

title: Binary Screenshot Transfer: PyVISA and Raw Socket Methods
body: Transferring screenshot binary data from oscilloscope to PC. PyVISA method (recommended for reliability): Use visalib.read() directly instead of read_raw() which may timeout. Read in 65536-byte chunks: chunk, status = scope.visalib.read(scope.session, 65536). Check status: if status != pyvisa.constants.StatusCode.success_max_count_read then transfer is complete. Set scope.timeout = 5000 for chunk reads. Raw socket method: Set sock.settimeout(5). Read with sock.recv(65536) in loop. When socket.timeout exception occurs and data has been received, transfer is complete. Both methods: Data is raw bytes (NO IEEE 488.2 header). PNG starts with magic bytes 89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A. Strip trailing newline if present.

title: Screenshot Common Errors: UnicodeDecodeError, Timeout, Access Violation
body: Common screenshot errors and solutions: (1) UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xe5 — Cause: Using query('*OPC?') after FILESystem:READFile; PNG binary data is still in the buffer. Solution: Use time.sleep(1.0) instead of *OPC? after HARDCOPY START or SAVE:IMAGE. (2) OSError: access violation reading 0x... — Cause: FILESystem:READFile called too soon. Solution: Add time.sleep(1.0) after SAVE:IMAGe or HARDCOPY START. (3) Timeout on HARDCOPY:DATA? — Cause: Using HARDCOPY:DATA? on modern scope (MSO5/6). Solution: Use SAVE:IMAGE + FILESYSTEM method. (4) HARDCOPY:FORMAT ${format} — Cause: Bug in code generator with template literals. Solution: Use proper string interpolation.

title: Command Group: Waveform Transfer (27-41 commands)
body: Waveform Transfer commands transfer waveform data from instrument to controller. Key commands: DATa:SOURce {CH<x>|MATH<x>|REF<x>} — select waveform source. DATa:ENCdg {ASCII|RIBinary|SRIBinary|RFBinary|SRFBinary} — set data encoding. DATa:WIDth {1|2|4|8} — bytes per data point. DATa:STARt <NR1> — first data point index. DATa:STOP <NR1> — last data point index. WFMOutpre? — query waveform preamble (scaling info). CURVe? — transfer waveform data. Preamble fields: YMUlt (vertical scale factor), YOFf (vertical offset), YZEro (vertical zero), XINcr (horizontal increment), XZEro (horizontal zero). Conversion: voltage = (raw_ADC - YOFf) * YMUlt + YZEro.

title: Command Group: Math Waveform (1-85 commands)
body: Math commands create and define math waveforms. Key commands: MATH<x>:DEFine "expression" — define math expression (e.g., 'CH1+CH2', 'CH1*CH2', 'FFT(CH1)'). MATH<x>:TYPe {BASic|FFT|ADVanced} — set math type. MATH<x>:SOURce1 CH<x> — set first source. MATH<x>:SOURce2 CH<x> — set second source (for dual-source operations). MATH<x>:FFT:WINdow {RECTangular|HAMming|HANning|BLACkmanharris} — set FFT window. MATH<x>:VERTical:SCAle <NR3> — set vertical scale. MATH<x>:VERTical:POSition <NR3> — set vertical position. On legacy scopes: MATHVAR:VAR<x> — set math variable. Math waveforms can be used as sources for measurements, triggers, and other math operations.

title: Command Group: Cursor (38-121 commands)
body: Cursor commands control cursor display and readout. Key commands: CURSor:FUNCtion {OFF|HBArs|VBArs|SCREEN|WAVEform} — set cursor type. CURSor:SOUrce {CH<x>|MATH<x>|REF<x>} — set cursor source. CURSor:VBArs:POSition1 <NR3> — set vertical bar 1 position. CURSor:VBArs:POSition2 <NR3> — set vertical bar 2 position. CURSor:HBArs:POSition1 <NR3> — set horizontal bar 1 position. CURSor:VBArs:DELta? — query delta between vertical bars. CURSor:HBArs:DELta? — query delta between horizontal bars. CURSor:WAVEform:POSition1 <NR3> — set waveform cursor position. Cursor readouts provide voltage and time measurements at cursor positions.

title: Command Group: Search and Mark (357-650 commands)
body: Search and Mark is the LARGEST command group (up to 650 commands). It provides waveform search capabilities. Key commands: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:TYPe {EDGE|PULSEWidth|TIMEOUT|RUNT|...} — set search type. SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:EDGE:SOURce CH<x> — set search source. SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:EDGE:SLOPe {RISe|FALL|EITher} — set edge slope. SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:LEVel <NR3> — set search level. MARK:CREATE — create a mark at current position. MARK:DELEte — delete a mark. MARK:NEXT — go to next mark. The group includes extensive bus protocol search (CAN, I2C, SPI, USB, etc.) mirroring the trigger command structure.

title: Command Group: Power, DPM, IMDA, WBG (26-268 commands)
body: Power analysis command groups cover multiple specialized measurement domains. Power (268 cmds): Control loop response, impedance, efficiency, harmonics, switching loss, ripple, SOA (Safe Operating Area), turn-on/turn-off measurements. Key: POWer:POWer<x>:TYPe {EFFICIENCY|HARMONICS|SWITCHINGLOSS|...}. DPM — Digital Power Management (26 cmds): Requires option 5-DPM or 6-DPM. Key: DPM:MEAS<x>:... IMDA — Inverter Motors and Drive Analysis (81 cmds): Input analysis, output analysis, ripple measurements for motor drive applications. WBG — Wide Band Gap (47 cmds): WBG-DPT (Wide Band Gap Device Power Test) for GaN/SiC device characterization.

title: Command Group: Mask Test and Limit Test
body: Mask commands (29-85 cmds) compare waveforms to standard or user-defined masks. Key commands: MASK:TYPe {STANDARD|CUSTOM} — set mask type. MASK:MASK<x>:SOURce CH<x> — set mask source. MASK:TESt:STATus? — query pass/fail status. MASK:AUTOAdjust — auto-adjust mask margins. MASK:MASK<x>:HDELTA/VDELTA — set horizontal/vertical tolerance. MASK:COUNt:TOTal? — query total waveform count. MASK:COUNt:HITS? — query hit count. Limit Test (22 cmds): LIMit:BEEP {ON|OFF} — beep on fail. LIMit:COMpare — start comparison. LIMit:COMpare:RESUlt? — query result. LIMit:TEMPlate:SOURce — set template source. Both groups support Act On Event actions for automated responses.

title: Command Groups: Alias, Callout, Miscellaneous, Status, Calibration
body: Alias (7 cmds): Define custom command shortcuts. ALIas:CATalog? — list aliases. ALIas:DEFine "name","command_sequence" — create alias. ALIas:DELEte:NAME "name" — delete alias. ALIas:STATE {ON|OFF} — enable/disable aliases. Callout (14 cmds): Create custom text annotations. CALLout:CALLOUT<x>:TEXT "text" — set callout text. CALLout:CALLOUT<x>:COLOR — set color. Miscellaneous (71 cmds): *IDN?, *RST, *OPC?, *CLS, *ESR?, *SRE, *STB?, HEADer {ON|OFF}, VERBose {ON|OFF}, LOCk {ALL|NONe}, UNLock ALL. Status and Error (17 cmds): *ESR?, ALLEV?, EVENT?, EVENTlog? Calibration (8 cmds): CALibrate:INTERNal — run signal path calibration. CALibrate:RESults? — query calibration status.

title: Command Groups: Spectrum View, Zoom, Histogram, Plot, DVM
body: Spectrum View (52 cmds): Control spectrum analysis display. SPECTRUMView:VIEW1:CH<x>:STATE {ON|OFF} — enable spectrum view. SPECTRUMView:VIEW1:CH<x>:RBW <NR3> — set resolution bandwidth. Zoom (20-27 cmds): ZOOm:ZOOm1:STATE {ON|OFF} — enable zoom. ZOOm:ZOOm1:HORizontal:SCAle <NR3> — set zoom horizontal scale. ZOOm:ZOOm1:HORizontal:POSition <NR3> — set zoom position. Histogram (10-28 cmds): HIStogram:BOX — set histogram box. HIStogram:MODe {HORizontal|VERTical|OFF} — set mode. Plot (47 cmds): PLOTView:PLOT<x>:TYPe {XY|BATHTUB|EYE|SPECTRUM|TREND|...} — set plot type. DVM (12 cmds): DVM:SOURce {CH<x>} — set DVM source. DVM:MEASUrement:VALue? — query DVM reading. DVM requires option (free with registration).

title: Command Groups: Act On Event, SaveOn, Digital, Ethernet, AFG, History
body: Act On Event (32 cmds): Program automated responses to trigger/search/measurement/mask events. ACTONEVent:TRIGger:ACTION:SAVEIMAGe:STATE {ON|OFF} — save image on trigger. ACTONEVent:SEARCH:ACTION — act on search event. SaveOn (8 cmds): Legacy automated save. SAVEON:FILe:AUTOInc {ON|OFF} — auto-increment filenames. Digital (33 cmds): DISplay:DIGital:HEIght — set digital channel display height. Requires digital probe on super channel. Ethernet (14 cmds): ETHERnet:DHCP {ON|OFF}. ETHERnet:IPADDress. ETHERnet:GATEWay. AFG (18 cmds): Built-in function generator option. AFG:OUTput:STATe {ON|OFF}. AFG:FUNCtion {SINE|SQUARE|PULSE|RAMP|NOISE|DC|...}. History (3 cmds): HIStory:STATE {ON|OFF}.

title: SCPI Command Groups Overview: 34 Groups, ~2952 Commands
body: The MSO 4/5/6/7 Series Programmer Manual defines 34 command groups containing approximately 2,952 total commands. Groups by size (approximate): Search and Mark (650) — largest group, extensive bus protocol search. Measurement (367) — automated measurements. Bus (339) — serial protocol configuration. Trigger (266) — trigger setup. Power (268) — power analysis. Display (130) — display control. Cursor (121) — cursor readout. Math (85) — math waveforms. Mask (85) — mask testing. IMDA (81) — motor drive analysis. Miscellaneous (71) — system commands. Horizontal (54) — time base. Spectrum (52) — spectrum analysis. WBG (47) — wide band gap. Plot (47) — plot types. Waveform Transfer (41) — data extraction. Digital (33) — digital channels. Smaller groups: ACT On Event (32), Histogram (28), Zoom (20), DPM (26), Save/Recall (26), Limit (22), File System (19), AFG (18), Acquisition (15), Status (17), Callout (14), Ethernet (14), DVM (12), Self Test (10), SaveOn (8), Calibration (8), Alias (7), History (3).

title: ACQuire:ENHANCEDEnob — Enhanced Effective Number of Bits
body: Command: ACQuire:ENHANCEDEnob {OFF|AUTO}. Query: ACQuire:ENHANCEDEnob? Sets or queries the state of enhanced effective number of bits (ENOB). Arguments: OFF = turns off enhanced ENOB. AUTO = turns on enhanced ENOB automatically. Related query: ACQuire:ENHANCEDEnob:STATE? returns 0 (off) or 1 (on). Example: ACQUIRE:ENHANCEDENOB AUTO — enables enhanced ENOB. Group: Acquisition. Family: MSO/DPO 5000/B, DPO 7000/C, DPO/MSO 70000 series.

title: EXPort:FILEName — Set Export File Path (Legacy Scopes)
body: Command: EXPort:FILEName <file path>. Query: EXPort:FILEName? Sets or queries the file path for the next EXPort command. The <file path> is a quoted string (max 128 characters) in the form <drive>:<dir>/<filename>. If no path specified, defaults to 'C:\Users\Public\Tektronix\TekScope\Screen Captures'. Extensions are recommended but not required. Example set: EXPORT:FILENAME "TEK.BMP" — sets TEK.BMP as export file. Example query: EXPORT:FILENAME? — returns current export path. Group: Hard copy. Family: MSO/DPO 5000/B, DPO 7000/C, DPO/MSO 70000 series.

title: EXPort:FORMat — Set Export Image Format (Legacy Scopes)
body: Command: EXPort:FORMat {BMP|JPEG|PCX|PNG|TIFF}. Query: EXPort:FORMat? Sets or queries the image format for exporting waveforms to a file. Arguments: BMP = bitmap format. JPEG = JPEG compressed. PCX = PCX format. PNG = Portable Network Graphics (recommended). TIFF = Tagged Image File Format. Example set: EXPORT:FORMAT PNG — sets export format to PNG. Example query: EXPORT:FORMAT? — might return 'JPEG'. Group: Hard copy. Family: MSO/DPO 5000/B, DPO 7000/C, DPO/MSO 70000 series.

title: HORizontal:SCAle and HORizontal:ACQDURATION — Time Base Control
body: HORizontal:SCAle <NR3> — sets the horizontal scale (time per division). Query: HORizontal:SCAle? Example: HORizontal:SCAle 1.0E-6 — set 1 microsecond/division. HORizontal:ACQDURATION? — query-only, returns total acquisition duration in seconds. HORizontal:ACQLENGTH? — query-only, returns total number of points in acquisition. HORizontal:RECOrdlength <NR1> — set record length (number of points). HORizontal:POSition <NR3> — set trigger position (percentage of record). Group: Horizontal. Family: MSO/DPO 5000/B, DPO 7000/C, DPO/MSO 70000 series.

title: DISplay Commands — Clock, Color, Graticule (Legacy Scopes)
body: DISplay:CLOCk {ON|OFF} — show/hide display clock. DISplay:COLOr? — query color settings. DISplay:GRAticule {FULL|GRID|CROSShair|FRAMe|IRE|NTSC} — set graticule type on legacy scopes. DISplay:PERSistence {OFF|INFPersist|VARpersist|AUTO} — set persistence mode. DISplay:STYle {DOTSonly|INTENSified|VECtors} — set waveform display style. DISplay:DIGital:HEIght — set digital channel display height. Group: Display control. Family: MSO/DPO 5000/B, DPO 7000/C, DPO/MSO 70000 series.

title: AFG: OUTPut:STATe — Enable/Disable Output Channel
body: Command: OUTPut{ch}:STATe {ON|OFF|0|1}. Query: OUTPut{ch}:STATe? Enables or disables the output signal on the specified AFG channel. When ON, the signal is present at the front panel connector. {ch} is 1 or 2 for dual-channel AFGs. Examples: afg.write('OUTPut1:STATe ON') — enable channel 1. state = afg.query('OUTPut1:STATe?') — query channel 1 state. Related: OUTPut{ch}:IMPedance {50|INFinity} — set output impedance. OUTPut{ch}:POLarity {NORMal|INVerted} — set polarity. Family: AFG31K, AFG3KB, AFG3KC, AFG3K.

title: AFG: SOURce:FUNCtion:SHAPe — Set Waveform Shape
body: Command: SOURce{ch}:FUNCtion:SHAPe {SINusoid|SQUare|PULSe|RAMP|PRNoise|DC|SINC|GAUSsian|LORentz|ERISe|EDECay|HAVersine|USER1|USER2|USER3|USER4}. Query: SOURce{ch}:FUNCtion:SHAPe? Sets the output waveform shape for the specified channel. Examples: afg.write('SOURce1:FUNCtion:SHAPe SINusoid') — sine wave. afg.write('SOURce1:FUNCtion:SHAPe SQUare') — square wave. afg.write('SOURce1:FUNCtion:SHAPe PULSe') — pulse. afg.write('SOURce1:FUNCtion:SHAPe RAMP') — ramp. afg.write('SOURce1:FUNCtion:SHAPe USER1') — arbitrary waveform 1. Related: SOURce{ch}:FUNCtion:RAMP:SYMMetry — ramp symmetry. SOURce{ch}:PULSe:DCYCle — duty cycle. Family: AFG31K, AFG3K.

title: AFG: Frequency, Amplitude, Offset, Phase Commands
body: SOURce{ch}:FREQuency:FIXed {freq} — set output frequency in Hz. Query: SOURce{ch}:FREQuency:FIXed? Range depends on model and waveform. SOURce{ch}:FREQuency:MODE {CW|SWEep} — set frequency mode (continuous wave or sweep). SOURce{ch}:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:AMPLitude {amplitude} — set peak-to-peak amplitude in Vpp. SOURce{ch}:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:OFFSet {offset} — set DC offset in volts. SOURce{ch}:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:HIGH {high} — set high voltage level. SOURce{ch}:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:LOW {low} — set low voltage level. SOURce{ch}:PHASe:ADJust {phase} — set phase in degrees. Examples: afg.write('SOURce1:FREQuency:FIXed 1E6') — 1 MHz. afg.write('SOURce1:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:AMPLitude 2.0') — 2 Vpp. Family: AFG31K, AFG3K.

title: TekExpress: SELECT Commands — Device, Suite, Test Configuration
body: TEKEXP:SELECT DEVICE,"<DeviceName>" — select the DUT (Device Under Test). Query: TEKEXP:SELECT? DEVICE returns current device name. TEKEXP:SELECT SUITE,"<SuiteName>" — select test suite. Query: TEKEXP:SELECT? SUITE returns current suite. TEKEXP:SELECT TEST,"<TestName>",<0|1> — enable (1) or disable (0) a specific test. Query: TEKEXP:SELECT? TEST returns all test names with enable/disable state. TEKEXP:LIST? DEVICE — list available devices. TEKEXP:LIST? SUITE — list available suites for selected device. TEKEXP:LIST? TEST — list available tests. TEKEXP:LIST? VERSION — list available versions. Family: TekExpress (USB4Tx, PCIe, Ethernet compliance).

title: TekExpress: STATE, RESULT, INSTRUMENT, VALUE Commands
body: TEKEXP:STATE RUN — start test execution. TEKEXP:STATE? — query current run state. TEKEXP:RESULT? — query test results after completion. TEKEXP:VALUE GENERAL,"<ParameterName>","<Value>" — set general parameter values. Query: TEKEXP:VALUE? GENERAL,"<ParameterName>" returns current value. TEKEXP:INSTRUMENT,"<InstrumentType>","<IPAddress>" — set instrument IP address by type. Query: TEKEXP:INSTRUMENT? "<InstrumentType>" returns current IP. TEKEXP:LIST? INSTRUMENT,"<InstrumentType>" — list available instruments of specified type. Typical workflow: (1) SELECT DEVICE → (2) SELECT SUITE → (3) SELECT TEST → (4) VALUE GENERAL (configure) → (5) INSTRUMENT (connect) → (6) STATE RUN → (7) STATE? (poll) → (8) RESULT? Family: TekExpress.

title: ACQuire:STATE — Start/Stop Acquisitions (Modern MSO)
body: Command: ACQuire:STATE {RUN|STOP|1|0}. Query: ACQuire:STATE? Starts or stops acquisitions. RUN or 1 = start acquiring. STOP or 0 = stop acquiring. When STOPAfter is set to SEQuence, starting acquisition collects the specified number of acquisitions then stops. Example set: scope.write('ACQuire:STATE RUN'). Example query: state = scope.query('ACQuire:STATE?') returns '1' or '0'. tm_devices: scope.commands.acquire.state.write(1). Related: ACQuire:STOPAfter, ACQuire:MODe, ACQuire:NUMAVg. Group: Acquisition. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6, MSO4B, MSO5B, MSO6B.

title: ACQuire:MODe — Set Acquisition Mode (Modern MSO)
body: Command: ACQuire:MODe {SAMple|PEAKdetect|HIRes|AVErage|ENVelope}. Query: ACQuire:MODe? Sets the acquisition mode. SAMple = real-time samples. PEAKdetect = captures min/max in each interval (glitch detection). HIRes = hardware averaging for higher resolution. AVErage = average over multiple acquisitions (set count with ACQuire:NUMAVg). ENVelope = cumulative min/max over multiple acquisitions. Example: scope.write('ACQuire:MODe AVErage'); scope.write('ACQuire:NUMAVg 64'). Group: Acquisition. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: CH<x> Commands — Scale, Position, Coupling, Bandwidth (Modern MSO)
body: CH<x>:SCAle <NR3> — set vertical scale (volts/division). Example: CH1:SCAle 0.5 (500mV/div). CH<x>:POSition <NR3> — set vertical position in divisions. CH<x>:COUPling {DC|AC|GND} — set input coupling. CH<x>:BANdwidth <NR3> — set bandwidth limit in Hz. CH<x>:OFFSet <NR3> — set vertical offset in volts. CH<x>:LABel:NAMe "text" — set channel label. CH<x>:PROBEFunc:EXTAtten <NR3> — set external attenuation. CH<x>:TERmination <NR1> — set termination (50 or 1E6 ohms). Example workflow: scope.write('CH1:COUPling DC'); scope.write('CH1:SCAle 1.0'); scope.write('CH1:POSition 0'). Group: Vertical. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: HORizontal Commands — Scale, Record Length, Mode (Modern MSO)
body: HORizontal:SCAle <NR3> — set time/division. Example: HORizontal:SCAle 1E-6 (1 µs/div). HORizontal:RECOrdlength <NR1> — set record length. Example: HORizontal:RECOrdlength 10000000 (10M points). HORizontal:POSition <NR3> — set trigger position (% of record, 0-100). HORizontal:MODE {AUTO|MANual|CONStant} — AUTO adjusts record length to maintain resolution. HORizontal:MODE:SAMPLERate <NR3> — set sample rate in Hz. HORizontal:ACQDURATION? — query total acquisition time. HORizontal:ACQLENGTH? — query total acquisition points. Group: Horizontal. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: TRIGger:A Commands — Edge Trigger Setup (Modern MSO)
body: TRIGger:A:TYPe EDGE — set trigger type to edge. TRIGger:A:EDGE:SOURce {CH<x>|AUX|LINE} — set trigger source. TRIGger:A:EDGE:SLOPe {RISe|FALL|EITher} — set trigger slope. TRIGger:A:LEVel:CH<x> <NR3> — set trigger level in volts. TRIGger:A:MODe {AUTO|NORMal} — AUTO triggers without signal, NORMal waits for trigger condition. TRIGger:FORCe — force a trigger event immediately. Example workflow: scope.write('TRIGger:A:TYPe EDGE'); scope.write('TRIGger:A:EDGE:SOURce CH1'); scope.write('TRIGger:A:EDGE:SLOPe RISe'); scope.write('TRIGger:A:LEVel:CH1 1.5'). Group: Trigger. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: MEASUrement Commands — Add, Configure, Read Measurements (Modern MSO)
body: MEASUrement:ADDNew "MEAS<x>" — add new measurement slot. MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:TYPe {FREQuency|PERIod|MEAN|PK2Pk|RMS|RISe|FALL|AMPlitude|HIGH|LOW|MAXimum|MINImum|PHASE|DELAY|...} — set measurement type. MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:SOUrce CH<x> — set measurement source. MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:VALue? — query instantaneous measurement result. MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:CURRentacq:MEAN? — query mean for current acquisition. MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:ALLAcqs:MEAN? — query mean across all acquisitions. MEASUrement:DELete "MEAS<x>" — delete a measurement. Example: scope.write('MEASUrement:ADDNew "MEAS1"'); scope.write('MEASUrement:MEAS1:TYPe FREQuency'); scope.write('MEASUrement:MEAS1:SOUrce CH1'). Group: Measurement. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: DISplay Commands — Show/Hide Channels, Graticule (Modern MSO)
body: DISplay:WAVEView1:CH<x>:STATE {ON|OFF} — show or hide a channel in waveview. DISplay:GRAticule:TYPe {FULL|GRID|CROSShair|FRAMe} — set graticule type. DISplay:PERSistence {OFF|INFPersist|VARpersist} — set persistence. DISplay:WAVEform {ON|OFF} — show/hide all waveforms. DISplay:REFERence {ON|OFF} — show/hide reference waveforms. DISplay:INTENSITy:WAVEform <NR1> — set waveform intensity (1-100). DISplay:STYle:DOTSonly {ON|OFF} — dots-only display. Example: scope.write('DISplay:WAVEView1:CH1:STATE ON'); scope.write('DISplay:WAVEView1:CH2:STATE OFF'). Group: Display. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: SAVE:IMAGe Commands — Full Detail (Modern MSO 4/5/6)
body: SAVE:IMAGe "<filepath>" — save screenshot to file on scope. Format determined by extension: .png, .bmp, .jpg. The filepath is a quoted string, max 128 chars. SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition {NORMal|INVErted} — NORMal = dark background (default), INVErted = white background (ink-saver). Query: SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition? SAVE:IMAGe:VIEWTYpe {FULLScreen|GRATicule|FULLNOmenu} — FULLScreen captures entire display including menus. Query: SAVE:IMAGe:VIEWTYpe? Python example: scope.write('SAVE:IMAGe:VIEWTYpe FULLScreen'); scope.write('SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition NORMal'); scope.write('SAVE:IMAGe "C:/Temp/capture.png"'); opc = scope.query('*OPC?'). Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: BUS Commands — CAN, I2C, SPI Configuration (Modern MSO)
body: BUS:B<x>:TYPe {CAN|LIN|SPI|I2C|RS232C|FLEXRAY|USB|ETHERNET|...} — set bus type. CAN setup: BUS:B1:TYPe CAN; BUS:B1:CAN:BITRate 500000; BUS:B1:CAN:SOURce CH1; BUS:B1:CAN:STANDARD {CAN2X|CANFD}. I2C setup: BUS:B1:TYPe I2C; BUS:B1:I2C:SCLk:SOURce CH1; BUS:B1:I2C:SDAta:SOURce CH2; BUS:B1:I2C:ADDRess:MODe {ADDR7|ADDR10}. SPI setup: BUS:B1:TYPe SPI; BUS:B1:SPI:SCLk:SOURce CH1; BUS:B1:SPI:SS:SOURce CH2; BUS:B1:SPI:DATA:IN:SOURce CH3; BUS:B1:SPI:BITOrder {MSB|LSB}. Group: Bus. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: SAVE/RECAll Commands — Setup and Waveform Save/Recall (Modern MSO)
body: SAVE:SETUp "<filepath>" — save current instrument setup to file. RECAll:SETUp "<filepath>" — recall instrument setup from file. SAVE:WAVEform CH<x>,"<filepath>" — save waveform data to file. SAVE:WAVEform:FILEFormat {INTERNal|SPREADSheet} — set waveform save format. SAVE:EVENTtable:BUS<x> "<filepath>" — save bus decode table. RECAll:WAVEform "<filepath>",REF<x> — recall waveform to reference. SAVE:SETUp file format: .set (internal). SAVE:WAVEform file formats: .wfm (internal), .csv (spreadsheet). Setup files preserve ALL instrument settings including trigger, measurements, bus decode. Group: Save and Recall. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: FILESystem Commands — Full Detail (Modern MSO)
body: FILESystem:CWD "<path>" — set current working directory. FILESystem:CWD? — query current directory. FILESystem:DIR? — list directory contents. FILESystem:MKDIR "<path>" — create directory. FILESystem:READFile "<path>" — read file from scope (returns raw binary, NO IEEE 488.2 header). FILESystem:WRITEFile "<path>",<block> — write file to scope. FILESystem:DELEte "<path>" — delete file. FILESystem:COPy "<source>","<dest>" — copy file. FILESystem:REName "<old>","<new>" — rename file. Common paths: C:/Temp (screenshots), C:/Users/Public/Tektronix/TekScope (default saves). CRITICAL: READFile returns raw bytes — use binary read methods, not text queries. Group: File System. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: DATa/CURVe Commands — Waveform Data Transfer (Modern MSO)
body: DATa:SOURce {CH<x>|MATH<x>|REF<x>|BUS<x>} — select data source. DATa:ENCdg {ASCII|RIBinary|SRIBinary|RFBinary|SRFBinary} — set encoding format. ASCII = human-readable comma-separated. RIBinary = signed big-endian binary (most common). SRIBinary = signed little-endian. DATa:WIDth {1|2|4|8} — bytes per data point. DATa:STARt <NR1> — first point (1-based). DATa:STOP <NR1> — last point. WFMOutpre? — query preamble (scaling info): BYT_Nr, BIT_Nr, ENCdg, BN_Fmt, BYT_Or, NR_Pt, XINcr, XZEro, PT_Off, YMUlt, YOFf, YZEro, XUNIT, YUNIT. CURVe? — transfer data. Convert: voltage = (raw - YOFf) * YMUlt + YZEro; time = (point_index - PT_Off) * XINcr + XZEro. Group: Waveform Transfer. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: AFG: Modulation, Burst, Sweep Commands
body: Modulation commands: SOURce{ch}:AM:STATe {ON|OFF} — enable/disable AM. SOURce{ch}:AM:DEPTh {percent} — set AM depth. SOURce{ch}:FM:STATe {ON|OFF} — enable/disable FM. SOURce{ch}:FM:DEViation {freq} — set FM deviation in Hz. SOURce{ch}:PM:STATe {ON|OFF} — enable/disable phase modulation. Burst commands: SOURce{ch}:BURSt:STATe {ON|OFF} — enable burst mode. SOURce{ch}:BURSt:NCYCles {count} — set number of cycles per burst. SOURce{ch}:BURSt:TDELay {seconds} — set burst delay. Sweep commands: SOURce{ch}:FREQuency:MODE SWEep — enable sweep mode. SOURce{ch}:FREQuency:STARt {freq} — sweep start frequency. SOURce{ch}:FREQuency:STOP {freq} — sweep stop frequency. SOURce{ch}:SWEep:TIME {seconds} — sweep duration. Family: AFG31K, AFG3K.

title: AFG: Trigger and System Commands
body: Trigger commands: TRIGger:SEQuence:SOURce {TIMer|EXTernal|MANual} — set trigger source. TRIGger:SEQuence:SOURce? — query trigger source. TRIGger:SEQuence:TIMer {seconds} — set timer interval for timer trigger. TRIGger — force immediate trigger (manual mode). System commands: *IDN? — query identification (manufacturer, model, serial, firmware). *RST — reset to factory defaults. *OPC? — operation complete query. *CLS — clear status. SYSTem:ERRor? — query error queue (returns error code and message). SYSTem:ERRor:NEXT? — query next error. SYSTem:VERSion? — query SCPI version. DATA:CATalog? — list available data sources. Family: AFG31K, AFG3K.

title: AFG Complete Workflow: Generate 1 MHz Sine Wave
body: Complete workflow to generate a 1 MHz, 2 Vpp sine wave on AFG channel 1: (1) afg.write('*RST') — reset to defaults. (2) afg.write('SOURce1:FUNCtion:SHAPe SINusoid') — set sine wave. (3) afg.write('SOURce1:FREQuency:FIXed 1E6') — set 1 MHz frequency. (4) afg.write('SOURce1:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:AMPLitude 2.0') — set 2 Vpp amplitude. (5) afg.write('SOURce1:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:OFFSet 0') — set 0V DC offset. (6) afg.write('OUTPut1:STATe ON') — enable output. Verify: freq = afg.query('SOURce1:FREQuency:FIXed?'). amp = afg.query('SOURce1:VOLTage:LEVel:IMMediate:AMPLitude?'). state = afg.query('OUTPut1:STATe?'). Family: AFG31K, AFG3K.

title: TekExpress: LIST Commands — Query Available Options
body: TEKEXP:LIST? DEVICE — returns comma-separated list of available DUT devices. TEKEXP:LIST? SUITE — returns available test suites for the currently selected device. TEKEXP:LIST? TEST — returns available tests for the selected device/suite. TEKEXP:LIST? VERSION — returns available version names. TEKEXP:LIST? INSTRUMENT,"<InstrumentType>" — returns available instruments of the specified type (e.g., 'Oscilloscope', 'BERTScope'). These queries are essential for discovering available options before configuring a test run. All LIST queries return comma-separated strings. Family: TekExpress.

title: TekExpress Complete Workflow: Run Compliance Test
body: Complete TekExpress compliance test workflow: (1) TEKEXP:LIST? DEVICE — discover available devices. (2) TEKEXP:SELECT DEVICE,"USB4_DUT" — select DUT. (3) TEKEXP:LIST? SUITE — discover available suites. (4) TEKEXP:SELECT SUITE,"USB4_Tx_Compliance" — select suite. (5) TEKEXP:LIST? TEST — discover available tests. (6) TEKEXP:SELECT TEST,"Eye_Diagram",1 — enable specific test. (7) TEKEXP:VALUE GENERAL,"Data Rate","20G" — configure parameters. (8) TEKEXP:INSTRUMENT,"Oscilloscope","192.168.1.10" — set scope IP. (9) TEKEXP:STATE RUN — start execution. (10) Poll TEKEXP:STATE? until not 'RUNNING'. (11) TEKEXP:RESULT? — get results. Family: TekExpress.

title: TekExpress SCPI Syntax Differences from Oscilloscopes
body: TekExpress SCPI commands differ from standard oscilloscope commands in several ways: (1) All commands use the TEKEXP: prefix — there is no colon-separated hierarchy like oscilloscope commands. (2) Commands use comma-separated sub-commands instead of colon-separated mnemonics: TEKEXP:SELECT DEVICE,"name" (not TEKEXP:SELECT:DEVICE). (3) Query form uses TEKEXP:SELECT? DEVICE (question mark after SELECT, not after DEVICE). (4) String arguments are always quoted with double quotes. (5) Boolean values use 0/1 integers, not ON/OFF. (6) LIST queries use TEKEXP:LIST? (always query-only). (7) TekExpress runs as a separate application on a PC, not on the oscilloscope itself — it communicates with instruments via their own SCPI interfaces.

title: SCPI Parameter Type: Mnemonic Ranges (CH<x>, REF<x>, MATH<x>)
body: Mnemonic range parameters use <x> as a numeric placeholder with instrument-specific limits. Channel ranges by model: MSO44/MSO44B = CH1-CH4. MSO46B = CH1-CH6. MSO54/MSO54B = CH1-CH4. MSO56B = CH1-CH6. MSO58/MSO58B/MSO58LP = CH1-CH8. MSO64/MSO64B = CH1-CH4. MSO66B = CH1-CH6. MSO68B = CH1-CH8. LPD64 = CH1-CH4. Reference waveforms: REF1-REF4 (typically). Math waveforms: MATH1-MATH4 (typically). Measurements: MEAS1-MEAS8+ (varies). Buses: B1-B4 (typically). Digital channels: CH<x>_D<y> where y is 0-15 per digital probe. The <x> ranges are validated by the instrument; out-of-range values generate errors.

title: SCPI Common Enumeration Values: ON/OFF, Boolean, State
body: Common SCPI enumeration patterns across Tektronix instruments. Boolean/State: {ON|OFF} or {1|0} — many commands accept both forms. Trigger mode: {AUTO|NORMal}. Acquisition mode: {SAMple|PEAKdetect|HIRes|AVErage|ENVelope}. Coupling: {DC|AC|GND}. Slope: {RISe|FALL|EITher}. Encoding: {ASCII|RIBinary|SRIBinary|RFBinary|SRFBinary}. Format: {PNG|BMP|JPEG|TIFF}. Persistence: {OFF|INFPersist|VARpersist}. Graticule: {FULL|GRID|CROSShair|FRAMe}. Important: Enumerations are case-insensitive and can be abbreviated to the uppercase portion. 'SAMPLE', 'SAMple', 'SAMP', and 'sample' are all valid for SAMple.

title: Complete Oscilloscope Setup Workflow: Single Acquisition
body: Complete workflow for setting up a single acquisition on MSO 4/5/6: (1) scope.write('*RST') — reset to defaults. (2) scope.write('CH1:COUPling DC') — set DC coupling. (3) scope.write('CH1:SCAle 1.0') — set 1V/div. (4) scope.write('CH1:POSition 0') — center vertically. (5) scope.write('HORizontal:SCAle 1E-6') — set 1µs/div. (6) scope.write('TRIGger:A:TYPe EDGE') — edge trigger. (7) scope.write('TRIGger:A:EDGE:SOURce CH1') — trigger on CH1. (8) scope.write('TRIGger:A:EDGE:SLOPe RISe') — rising edge. (9) scope.write('TRIGger:A:LEVel:CH1 0.5') — 500mV level. (10) scope.write('ACQuire:STOPAfter SEQuence') — stop after one. (11) scope.write('ACQuire:STATE RUN') — start. (12) scope.query('*OPC?') — wait for completion. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: Complete Measurement Workflow: Frequency and Amplitude
body: Complete workflow for automated frequency and amplitude measurements: (1) scope.write('MEASUrement:ADDNew "MEAS1"') — add measurement 1. (2) scope.write('MEASUrement:MEAS1:TYPe FREQuency') — measure frequency. (3) scope.write('MEASUrement:MEAS1:SOUrce CH1') — from channel 1. (4) scope.write('MEASUrement:ADDNew "MEAS2"') — add measurement 2. (5) scope.write('MEASUrement:MEAS2:TYPe PK2Pk') — measure peak-to-peak. (6) scope.write('MEASUrement:MEAS2:SOUrce CH1') — from channel 1. (7) scope.write('ACQuire:STATE RUN') — acquire. (8) scope.query('*OPC?') — wait. (9) freq = scope.query('MEASUrement:MEAS1:VALue?') — read frequency. (10) pkpk = scope.query('MEASUrement:MEAS2:VALue?') — read pk-pk. Cleanup: scope.write('MEASUrement:DELete "MEAS1"'). Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: Complete Waveform Transfer Workflow with Scaling
body: Complete waveform data transfer and conversion: (1) scope.write('DATa:SOURce CH1'). (2) scope.write('DATa:ENCdg RIBinary'). (3) scope.write('DATa:WIDth 2'). (4) scope.write('DATa:STARt 1'). (5) reclen = int(scope.query('HORizontal:RECOrdlength?')). (6) scope.write(f'DATa:STOP {reclen}'). (7) preamble = scope.query('WFMOutpre?') — parse YMUlt, YOFf, YZEro, XINcr, XZEro, PT_Off. (8) raw = scope.query_binary_values('CURVe?', datatype='h', container=np.array) — 'h' for 2-byte signed. (9) voltage = (raw - YOFf) * YMUlt + YZEro. (10) time = np.arange(len(raw)) * XINcr + XZEro. Now voltage[] and time[] contain the full waveform in physical units. For 1-byte data: use DATa:WIDth 1 and datatype='b'. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: PyVISA Connection Setup for Tektronix Instruments
body: PyVISA connection setup for Tektronix oscilloscopes and generators: import pyvisa; rm = pyvisa.ResourceManager(). VISA resource strings: TCPIP socket: 'TCPIP0::<ip>::<port>::SOCKET' (e.g., 'TCPIP0::192.168.1.10::4000::SOCKET'). VXI-11: 'TCPIP0::<ip>::inst0::INSTR'. GPIB: 'GPIB0::<addr>::INSTR'. Setup: scope = rm.open_resource(resource_string). scope.timeout = 30000 (30 seconds). For SOCKET: scope.read_termination = '\n'; scope.write_termination = '\n'. For INSTR: termination handled by VISA driver. Test: idn = scope.query('*IDN?'). Always set appropriate timeout for long operations (screenshots: 60s, waveform transfer: 30s, calibration: 300s).

title: tm_devices Library: Alternative to Raw SCPI Commands
body: tm_devices is Tektronix's official Python library providing an object-oriented API as an alternative to raw SCPI strings. Connection: from tm_devices import DeviceManager; dm = DeviceManager(); scope = dm.add_scope('192.168.1.10'). Command mapping: scope.write('ACQuire:STATE RUN') becomes scope.commands.acquire.state.write('RUN'). scope.query('ACQuire:MODe?') becomes scope.commands.acquire.mode.query(). scope.write('CH1:SCAle 0.5') becomes scope.commands.ch[1].scale.write(0.5). scope.query('MEASUrement:MEAS1:VALue?') becomes scope.commands.measurement.meas[1].value.query(). Benefits: IDE autocomplete, type checking, automatic parameter validation. The SCPI command tree maps directly to the object hierarchy.

title: Robust SCPI Automation: Error Handling Best Practices
body: Best practices for robust SCPI automation: (1) Always use *CLS at start to clear error queue. (2) Check *ESR? after critical operations — non-zero means error occurred. (3) Read ALLEV? to get full error descriptions. (4) Use *OPC? after long operations (save, export, calibration) to ensure completion. (5) Set appropriate timeouts: normal commands 5s, saves/exports 30s, calibration 300s. (6) Use try/except around VISA operations for connection resilience. (7) After errors, send *CLS and possibly *RST to recover. (8) For screenshots, add time.sleep(1.0) between save and read to ensure file is written. (9) Validate query responses — empty or unexpected formats indicate issues. (10) Use scope.write('HEADer OFF') for easier response parsing (no header prefix).

title: Complete CAN Bus Decode and Trigger Workflow
body: Complete CAN bus decode and trigger setup: (1) scope.write('BUS:ADDNew "BUS1"') — add bus. (2) scope.write('BUS:BUS1:TYPe CAN') — set CAN type. (3) scope.write('BUS:BUS1:CAN:SOURce CH1') — CAN_H on CH1. (4) scope.write('BUS:BUS1:CAN:BITRate 500000') — 500 kbps. (5) scope.write('BUS:BUS1:CAN:STANDARD CAN2X') — CAN 2.0. (6) scope.write('DISplay:WAVEView1:BUS:BUS1:STATE ON') — display bus. Trigger on CAN ID: (7) scope.write('TRIGger:A:TYPe BUS'). (8) scope.write('TRIGger:A:BUS:SOURce BUS1'). (9) scope.write('TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:CONDition IDentifier'). (10) scope.write('TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:IDentifier:MODe STANDard'). (11) scope.write('TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:IDentifier:VALue "0x123"'). Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6.

title: Unified Screenshot Capture: Auto-Detect and Capture
body: Universal screenshot capture that auto-detects scope family: (1) idn = scope.query('*IDN?'). (2) Parse model from IDN (split by comma, check parts[1]). (3) If 'MSO4'/'MSO5'/'MSO6' or 'TEKSCOPESW' → use SAVE:IMAGe method. (4) If 'MSO7'/'DPO7' (5+ digits) → use EXPort START method. (5) If 'MSO5'/'DPO5' (4 digits) or 'DPO7' (4 digits) → use HARDCopy method. SAVE:IMAGe path: scope.write('SAVE:IMAGe "C:/Temp/shot.png"'). EXPort path: scope.write('EXPort:FILEName "C:/TekScope/shot.png"'); scope.write('EXPort:FORMat PNG'); scope.write('EXPort START'). HARDCopy path: data = scope.query_binary_values('HARDCopy:DATA?', datatype='B', container=bytes). All paths: wait with *OPC? then transfer with FILESystem:READFile if file-based.

title: Power Measurement Workflow: Efficiency and Harmonics
body: Power analysis measurement workflow (requires Power option): Efficiency measurement: (1) scope.write('POWer:ADDNew "Power1"'). (2) scope.write('POWer:POWer1:TYPe EFFICIENCY'). (3) scope.write('POWer:POWer1:EFFICIENCY:INPUTSOURce:VOLTage CH1'). (4) scope.write('POWer:POWer1:EFFICIENCY:INPUTSOURce:CURRent CH2'). (5) scope.write('POWer:POWer1:EFFICIENCY:OUTPUTSOURce:VOLTage CH3'). (6) scope.write('POWer:POWer1:EFFICIENCY:OUTPUTSOURce:CURRent CH4'). Harmonics: scope.write('POWer:POWer1:TYPe HARMONICS'); configure source and standard (IEC61000). Switching loss: scope.write('POWer:POWer1:TYPe SWITCHINGLOSS'); configure voltage/current sources and gate signal. Family: MSO4, MSO5, MSO6 (with power option).

title: SCPI Command JSON Schema: Required and Optional Fields
body: Standard JSON schema for SCPI command entries in the TekAutomate corpus. Required fields: id (unique string), category (string, e.g., 'acquisition'), scpi (full command string), header (command without arguments), commandType ('set'|'query'|'both'), shortDescription (brief text), description (full text), mnemonics (string array). Optional fields: instruments.families (string array), instruments.models (string array), arguments (array of {name, type, required, validValues}), queryResponse ({type, format, description, example}), syntax ({set, query, argumentType}), codeExamples (array with scpi/python/tm_devices), relatedCommands (string array), notes (string array), dynamicActivation, concatenation, backwardCompatibility.
